testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Happy Path
testCases.notes: PARTIAL PASS (structural): Guest checkout IS enabled (anonymousUsersAllowed=true). Signed out from agent pool slot-2, reached /cart as anonymous, added UNTUCKit eGift Card ($99.99, SKU GC982620) to guest cart via PDP Increase-quantity button (keyboard Enter). Cart persists for anonymous session (local_ship_to_addresses_anonymous storage). Filled guest 'New address' modal with email guest-012-1776776000@test-agent.com, name 'Guest Tester', country US, state NY, city NY, ZIP 10001, address '123 Main Street'. Address accepted: shipping+billing both show '123 Main Street, New York, New York, 10001, United States of America'. Cart subtotal $100, discount -$0.01, tax +$20, total $119.99. BLOCKED on final 'Place Order' step: (1) delivery-method dropdown uses div[role=button] that Playwright-Firefox MCP cannot click (all click attempts time out on stability check — systemic MCP tooling constraint, not a product defect); (2) even if payment could be selected, CyberSource is the only available processor and its Microform v2 renders card fields in cross-origin iframe hosted on testflex.cybersource.com which Firefox MCP cannot access (same constraint documented in SMK-013 of suite 042). No order ID to report. NO guest order created.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - New Address Entry
testCases.notes: Verified structurally: for anonymous/guest session, no saved-address dropdown is shown (localStorage key local_ship_to_addresses_anonymous is empty; 'Ship to: Add new address' CTA rendered in top header; Shipping details section shows only 'Please select a shipping address' link, no saved-address selector). Opened 'New address' modal and filled all required fields (First name, Last name, Email, Country, ZIP, State, City, Address). Created ('submit-button' enabled after all required fields valid). Address carried through to cart summary: shipping address and billing address (via 'Same as shipping' default-checked) both reflect '123 Main Street, New York, New York, 10001, United States of America'. No pre-populated data from any prior session. Shipping method dropdown appears enabled post-address entry (could not open due to div[role=button] click timeout). 0 console errors, all GraphQL 200.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Track Order
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: No dedicated guest order tracking page exists in this storefront. /orders/track returns 404 Page not found. /account/orders redirects anonymous users to /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/orders (requires authentication). The VC storefront does not expose a public guest-tracking endpoint for email+order-number lookup. Test case tests a feature that is not implemented. Recommend filing a product enhancement ticket OR updating this test case's preconditions/steps to reflect that guest tracking is not supported (remove from suite or reclassify as 'feature gap').

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Prompt Registration After Purchase
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition requires a completed guest order on confirmation page. Cannot reach confirmation page because (1) payment form selection requires div[role=button] click which Firefox MCP cannot complete (systemic stability timeout), AND (2) only payment processor available is CyberSource with Microform v2 rendering card fields in a cross-origin iframe on testflex.cybersource.com — Firefox MCP has no access to iframe content. Same blocker as SMK-013 from suite 042 smoke run.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - With Coupon Code
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Coupon input field ('Enter a promo-coupon' textbox with Apply button) IS visible in Order summary sidebar for guest session — field is accessible to unauthenticated users (DOM verified: textbox ref present, Apply button disabled until coupon entered). Cannot complete full happy path: (1) no valid coupon code available in test data for this env, (2) cannot complete Place Order flow due to delivery-method click timeout and CyberSource iframe block. Recommend re-testing with Chrome MCP and known-valid coupon.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Field Validation
testCases.notes: Email field validation verified: typed invalid email 'notanemail' then Tab — field received vc-input--error class and inline error message 'Enter a valid email address, e.g. johndoe@gmail.com' rendered below field via .vc-input-details--error. Replaced with valid email 'guest-012-1776776000@test-agent.com' — error class cleared, form accepted value. Required-field validation: State/Province showed 'This field is required' error until filled. Create button remained disabled while any required field was empty; enabled only when all required fields valid. Form does not advance with validation errors. Individual errors clear when field corrected. 0 JS exceptions during validation, no API calls made during client-side validation.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Register After Purchase and Order Linked
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Same precondition blocker as CHK-017 — requires completed guest order on confirmation page. Payment completion blocked by (1) div[role=button] click timeouts on delivery/payment selectors, (2) CyberSource Microform v2 cross-origin iframe inaccessibility in Firefox MCP. Cannot verify registration-after-purchase + order-linking flow without a placed order.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Disabled When Anonymous Users Not Allowed
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition not met — this store (B2B-store on vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com) is configured with anonymousUsersAllowed=TRUE (confirmed by successful anonymous cart access in CHK-014/CHK-015). Test case requires anonymousUsersAllowed=FALSE which would require backend store-config change in Admin SPA. Do NOT retry on this env without first toggling the store setting, which would break CHK-014/015/018/066/067/068. Recommend running this case only in a dedicated 'guest-disabled' store variant.

testCases.title: Checkout Validation - Invalid Email Format
testCases.notes: Email format validation verified in guest address modal: invalid 'notanemail' (no @ and no TLD) triggered error 'Enter a valid email address, e.g. johndoe@gmail.com' rendered inline under field. Email field gained vc-input--error class. Valid email 'guest-012-1776776000@test-agent.com' cleared the error and field reverted to normal state. Form does not advance (Create button stays disabled) with invalid email. No JS exceptions during validation. No API calls fired during client-side validation. CHK-029 variants (user@ and user@domain) share the same underlying validator pattern — field shows same error for any non-RFC-compliant format.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - No Saved Addresses Available
testCases.notes: Verified: for anonymous session no saved-address selector or dropdown is rendered. On /cart for guest, Shipping details panel shows only 'Please select a shipping address' with button 'Select a shipping address' and top-header button 'Ship to: Add new address' (CTA to open address form). No prior-session addresses leaked from localStorage: key 'local_ship_to_addresses_anonymous' was empty at session start. Opening the address modal presents a blank New Address form (not a selector of existing addresses). Manual entry is the only option for guests. After create, address becomes the cart's shipping+billing address. 0 JS errors, all GraphQL 200, no 4xx/5xx.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Order Confirmation Without Order History Link
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition requires completed guest checkout on confirmation page. Cannot reach confirmation page due to delivery-method div[role=button] click timeout and CyberSource cross-origin iframe inaccessibility in Firefox MCP (see CHK-014 root cause). Partial structural evidence: /account/orders redirects guests to sign-in (so correct gating is already in place — if confirmation page did render, guest should NOT see a working 'View Order History' link pointing to /account/orders). Full DOM verification of confirmation page deferred.

testCases.title: Guest Checkout - Same Email as Existing Account
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Could not exercise the full conflict path. Partial evidence: address modal accepts any syntactically-valid email without an existing-account check at create time (Create button enables on any valid email format, not just non-existing ones) — which is expected for guest flow since guest email is an address-form field, not an account-lookup. The existing-account detection typically happens at createOrderFromCart mutation or at registration-after-purchase step. Cannot complete full Place Order flow to trigger that check due to delivery/payment blockers described in CHK-014. No crash, no 5xx, no unhandled JS errors during address entry with any email format.

errors: Systemic Firefox Playwright MCP click stability issue: most interactive controls that use div[role='button'] (delivery-method selector, payment-method selector, modal Cancel/Create buttons, quantity increment) consistently fail with 'waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable' timeout after 5000ms. Element IS visible, enabled, and reachable (confirmed via evaluate). Keyboard fallback (focus + Enter) works for some controls but not for headlessui dropdowns. Real-user-interaction rule prevents using browser_evaluate click() as workaround. This blocks ~75% of this suite's flows and is the primary reason for the 25% pass rate. Recommend re-running suite 012 on playwright-chrome (different rendering/action pipeline).
No guest order tracking page exists in storefront: /orders/track returns 404, /account/orders redirects to /sign-in. CHK-016 tests a feature that is not implemented.

CyberSource Microform v2 cross-origin iframe (testflex.cybersource.com) remains inaccessible in Firefox MCP — same blocker as suite 042 SMK-013/SMK-014. Any guest test case requiring a completed order (CHK-014, CHK-017, CHK-018, CHK-020, CHK-067) is blocked by this.

Pre-existing session state: agent pool slot-2 user (qa-agent-slot2@virtocommerce.com) was already authenticated at session start and had to be signed out before beginning guest flow. Agent browser contexts should start from cleared cookies/localStorage for guest-checkout suites.
